name: Mariachi Beefballs and Rice
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix ground beef with corn chips, milk, egg and 2 tsp salt. Cover and refrigerate for 1 hour.
2. Shape the mixture into 15 meatballs, using about 2 rounded tablespoons of the mixture for each.
3. Lightly coat the meatballs in flour, making sure they are completely covered.
4. In a large frying pan, cook the meatballs in hot butter, half at a time, stirring until evenly browned. Remove meatballs as they are cooked.
5. Cook onion and garlic about 5 minutes in the same frying pan, stirring occasionally.
6. In a small bowl, mix remaining flour with salt, chili powder and cumin. Stir into onions.
7. Add tomatoes, green chilies and olives. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly; reduce heat, cover tightly and cook slowly for 30 minutes.
8. Add meatballs to the tomato mixture, cover tightly and cook slowly for another 20 minutes.
9. Uncover and continue cooking slowly for an additional 10 minutes.
10. Serve the meatballs and sauce over hot Mexican Rice.
Mexican Rice:
1. In a baking pan, layer cooked rice, sour cream-chili mixture and cheese strips. Repeat layers and top with remaining rice.
2. Bake in a moderate oven (350°F) for 25 minutes. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese and top with meatballs and sauce. Continue baking until cheese melts, about 5 minutes.
